compiled_state.title: As a developer, hooks/auto-format.sh reads commands.format to run stack-appropriate formatting on staged files
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify plugins/dso/hooks/auto-format.sh to read the commands.format config key. When present, invoke the configured formatter on staged files. When absent and .py files are staged, preserve legacy ruff behavior. When absent and no .py files are staged, emit [DSO WARN] and exit 0.

**Why**: Enables non-Python projects to use their native formatter (Prettier, RuboCop --autocorrect) via the PostToolUse hook.

**Scope**:
- IN: auto-format.sh hook changes; staged-file detection and invocation; legacy ruff fallback path for Python projects
- OUT: onboarding writes, other gate scripts

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, auto-format.sh with commands.format=prettier --write invokes prettier --write on staged files
  ← Satisfies: "auto-format.sh reads commands.format; when present, invokes configured formatter on staged files"
- When this story is complete, auto-format.sh with no commands.format key emits [DSO WARN] commands.format not configured and exits 0 (non-Python project case)
  ← Satisfies: "auto-format.sh emits [DSO WARN] when absent with exit 0"
- When this story is complete, auto-format.sh on a Python-only project with no commands.format key continues to invoke ruff on staged .py files (legacy behavior preserved) — verified by test
  ← Satisfies: "auto-format.sh emits [DSO WARN] when absent with exit 0" (Python legacy path must remain intact)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing covering: key present, key absent (non-Python WARN), key absent (Python legacy ruff fallback)

## Considerations

- [Consumer Impact] CLAUDE.md documents "Formatting runs automatically via PostToolUse hook on .py edits (ruff)." When commands.format is absent, auto-format.sh MUST preserve legacy ruff behavior on .py files — Python-only projects must not require re-onboarding to continue formatting after this story ships
- [Reliability] Existing Python-only projects on upgrade must continue to format .py files without requiring config migration
- [Testing] Three-state test: (a) commands.format present, (b) commands.format absent (non-Python → WARN), (c) commands.format absent (Python → legacy ruff)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
